items.title: Man faces court charged with murdering elderly mother in her Canberra home

children.content: Marcus Kauffman made a brief appearance in the ACT Magistrates Court this morning, where no bail application was made.

gallery.altText: A crime scene at a residential home in O'Connor.

thumbnail.altText: A crime scene at a residential home in O'Connor.

items.title: Why four lanes might not be better than two on this Canberra road

children.content: The latest federal budget has committed $50 million to duplicate a road in West Belconnen, but transport planning experts warn it could encourage even more peak-hour traffic.

image.altText: Cars travel along a main road past traffic lights. A large hill with a tower at the top is in the background.

items.title: Inquest into Cooma grandmother's death hears from relatives on final day

children.content: Family of Clare Nowland have addressed a coroners court on the final day of an inquest examining her death.

image.altText: an elderly woman looking at the camera

items.title: CIT chief told she could be fired for disclosing misconduct probe

children.content: Margot McNeill did not tell the Canberra Institute of Technology board she was under investigation for misconduct when she was being recruited.

gallery.altText: A professional in front of CIT signage

thumbnail.altText: A professional in front of CIT signage

items.title: 'Foldable' pistols, 3D-printed guns seized in raid 450m from police station

children.content: A police investigation has uncovered a large cache of weapons in the national capital's suburbs, less than a kilometre from the Belconnen Police Station.

image.altText: A long line of yellow, purple and white 3D-printed firearms.

items.title: Long-awaited RSPCA facility given funding boost in federal budget

children.content: Other funding boosts for the ACT revealed in the budget papers include $50 million to duplicate Drake Brockman Drive in West Belconnen, aimed at servicing the area's growing population, and almost $10 million for the National Film and Sound Archive.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses laughs at the camera while holding a dog

items.title: Technicolour frogs released into the wild near Canberra to bolster numbers

children.content: More than 800 tiny, technicoloured northern corroboree frogs are released into Brindabella National Park to try to bolster the numbers of one of Australia's most critically endangered amphibians.

gallery.altText: An up close of the frogs, with black and yellow markings, in Del Leong's palm.
An up close of the Northern Corroboree frog, with black and yellow markings, in an enclosure.
A tiny black and yellow frog released into the wild, grass.

thumbnail.altText: An up close of the frogs, with black and yellow markings, in Del Leong's palm.
An up close of the Northern Corroboree frog, with black and yellow markings, in an enclosure.
A tiny black and yellow frog released into the wild, grass.

items.title: The United States and China just made a pact

children.content: This week in Beijing, Donald Trump and Xi Jinping quietly admitted something the world has been slow to grasp: neither can afford a collision between their two countries.

articles.title: No major Iran war breakthroughs as Trump leaves Beijing

children.content: US President Donald Trump left China on Friday without securing tangible help from Beijing to end the Iran war, but praised China's President, Xi Jinping, during his visit.

image.altText: Chinese President Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump walk in blue suits in front of five American and Chinese flags.

gallery.altText: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping shake hands while posing for photos.

thumbnail.altText: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping shake hands while posing for photos.

items.title: Wayne Swan slams 'view from Mar-a-Lago' as Labor faces another test

children.content: Labor and the Coalition are both acutely mindful of One Nation's rise and its determination to take a decisive role in a future conservative government.

image.altText: A man with glasses and grey hair

items.title: Kalgoorlie's 'shovel ready' water project misses out in federal budget

children.content: A project designed to drought-proof Australia's biggest outback city has missed out on funding in this week's federal budget, with the City of Kalgoorlie-Boulder set to review its ambitious plans for what has been dubbed Water Bank.

image.altText: A drone photograph of Kalgoorlie-Boulder in regional Australia.

items.title: 'The sword is still hanging': Farmers in limbo as Inland Rail land sits idle

children.content: For more than a decade, Australians in the path of the proposed Inland Rail corridor have wondered if the nation-building project will force them from their homes. Although the northern section has been scrapped, the legacy of its blueprints is still fuelling anxiety.

gallery.altText: a man with hand on hip stands in front of his brown soil
a man with hand on hip stands in front of his brown soil

thumbnail.altText: a man with hand on hip stands in front of his brown soil
a man with hand on hip stands in front of his brown soil

items.title: Scottish ref under police protection as Celtic penalty sparks threats

children.content: Scottish FA condemns "vigilantism" after a referee's personal details are leaked online following a highly controversial penalty in the Scottish Premier League.

image.altText: A Scottish football referee runs back from the sideline pointing towards the penalty spot as players watch.

items.title: Demons seek top six spot against Hawks after Crows demolish Roos

children.content: The Demons test themselves at the MCG against a Hawthorn side desperate to bounce back, after the Crows demolish the Kangaroos at Adelaide Oval. Follow live.

image.altText: Kade Chandler handballs under pressure from three Hawthorn players

items.title: Where to watch Delta Goodrem's Eurovision final performance

children.content: The Eurovision Song Contest is on this Sunday, which will mean an early start for superfans of the pop culture phenomenon.

Delta Goodrem sings while standing on top of a tall podium coming out of a gold piano

items.title: Jane Caro reckons Australia is kidding itself about private schools

children.content: "There are absolutely no statistics that say that because you sent your kid to a private school they're going to do better in life."

image.altText: Jane Caro

items.title: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know

children.content: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.

image.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

items.title: A little box of joy came into my life at just the right time

children.content: This little box of joy is a microcosm, a mini-exemplar of what a small amount of local effort can do to help fix some of our damage of the natural world.

gallery.altText: Lapis the bluetit in her nest

thumbnail.altText: Lapis the bluetit in her nest

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: A hearty slow-cooked beef stew that will warm you up as the weather cools

children.content: Just 15 minutes of prep means your slow cooker will have a rich beef stew waiting at dinner time.

image.altText: A dinner bowl filled with rice and beef stew sits on a bench.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of patron saints, the Friends finale and a 90s club banger

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

image.altText: A dog is blessed by a priest in Spain on Saint Anthony's Day.

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship Australians unlikely to be paid out, experts say

children.content: But the lawyer who led the class action in the COVID-19 era Ruby Princess case has warned the cruise company will likely argue Australian law had no jurisdiction over the matter.

gallery.altText: A group of people in full body protective gear are in a minibus

thumbnail.altText: A group of people in full body protective gear are in a minibus

items.title: Police make arrest over alleged stabbing of man sleeping at home in NT town

children.content: A 23-year-old man has been arrested over the alleged stabbing of another man who was sleeping in a home in remote Tennant Creek about 990 kilometres south-east of Darwin.

image.altText: A close-up shows flashing lights at night time on top of a police car.

items.title: 'Incompetent': Action taken against Latham after online outburst

children.content: Contempt proceedings have been launched against Mark Latham after he attacked a tribunal that found he unlawfully vilified and sexually harassed another state MP.

image.altText: Mark Latham in a black coat and orange time walking

items.title: Voters flock to booths as Stafford by-election polls open

children.content: Labor candidate Luke Richmond wants to make the contest a referendum on health, while LNP candidate Fiona Hammond is focused on fuel, the cost of living, and crime.

image.altText: A graphic showing Fiona Hammond and Luke Richmond with blue and red in the background as well as Brisbane's skyline.

items.title: The southern Sydney bay residents say is becoming a mudflat

children.content: A bin that has been sticking out of the mud for 12 years has become a symbol for an ongoing issue of sediment build-up in Oyster Bay in Sydney's Sutherland Shire.

image.altText: a man standing by the shore of a shallow bay

items.title: A Chinese undersea device found near Bali may be part of something much bigger

children.content: The discovery of a Chinese undersea monitoring device in a strategic waterway "shows the extent of both China's reach and its undersea ambitions" and has implications for Australia's defence strategy, say experts.

gallery.altText: A graphic showing an ocean cutaway with satellites, buouys, drones and sensors trying to detect a submarine.
A torpedo-like object in the shallows at a beach.
A white Chinese research vessel at dock with a city in the background.

items.title: First north-west cloudband of 2026 to soak all states

children.content: The most widespread May rain in a decade is about to sweep across Australia as the first north-west cloudband of 2026 becomes a reality.

image.altText: heavy dark clouds over the Clarence River in the NSW North Coast

items.title: Amid grief, the NT's CLP rushes drastic child protection changes

children.content: Stakeholders have just one week to respond to sweeping changes to NT child protection laws that Aboriginal groups say could do "generations" of harm.

image.altText: A woman writes on a poster by candlelight

items.title: 'Recipe for disaster': Road safety expert 'not surprised' by fatal Qld bus crash

children.content: A retired traffic engineer says Australia is "decades behind" in road safety standards and needs an overhaul after a second fatal bus crash on the same stretch of the Bruce Highway.

image.altText: A green bus on its side in grass after a crash. Emergency service workers are seen on the right hand side.

items.title: Frustrated Scheffler slams 'absurd' pin positions after disappointing round

children.content: Scottie Scheffler takes issue with the pin positions at the PGA Championship after being upstaged by an American duo.

image.altText: Scottie Scheffler crosses his arms while holding a golf club

items.title: Video of 4m tiger shark beheading prompts call for change

children.content: A researcher speaks out against the fishing of sharks after footage of a huge tiger shark being caught and decapitated circulated on social media.

thumbnail.altText: hands holding large shark's mouth open.

items.title: Judge declares another mistrial in Harvey Weinstein rape case

children.content: Harvey Weinstein's third trial in New York on a charge that he raped aspiring actress Jessica Mann has ended in a mistrial, after a jury failed to reach a unanimous verdict.

image.altText: A man in a suit and glasses with facial hair sits in a courtroom

items.title: Neo-Nazis fundraising to challenge laws that listed them as hate group

children.content: The leader of Neo-Nazi group National Socialist Network, Thomas Sewell, has appeared in an interview online alongside notorious US Neo-Nazi James Mason, who is listed as a terrorist entity by the Canadian government.

image.altText: Shadowy figures in the foreground on an orange background with an Australian flag and US flag

items.title: Couple's warning after losing everything, including two dogs, in devastating fire

children.content: While Patrick and Susan Doyle escaped with their lives after the fire on South Australia's Limestone Coast, Mrs Doyle was badly burnt and their dogs, Mercy and Oscar, perished. They are warning others to be better prepared.

items.title: Israel targets Hamas leader, kills seven Palestinians

children.content: Seven Palestinians, including a child, have died in Friday's air strikes, which Israel said targeted Hamas militant leader Izz al-Din al-Haddad.

image.altText: A grey multi-level building shows damaged walls and windows as fire burns inside.

items.title: Zelenskyy vows retribution against Russia for missile strike that killed 24

children.content: Russia has unleashed its heaviest bombardment of the Ukrainian capital this year after a US-brokered ceasefire ended.

image.altText: A woman cries as she lays flowers at a memorial.
